Title: Different viral effectors hijack TCP17, a key transcription factor for host Auxin synthesis, to promote viral infection

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during TSWV infection; viral suppressor/effector protein encoded by TSWV S RNA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid or disrupt NSs–TCP17 interaction to preserve host auxin biosynthesis and antiviral defense; engineering host alleles of TCP17 insensitive to NSs may improve resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NSs is a viral effector rather than a host metabolic gene; it suppresses auxin accumulation indirectly by binding TCP17 and disrupting TCP17 homodimerization, without directly affecting TCP17 DNA binding to YUC5 promoter; NSs mutants defective in TCP17 interaction reduce TSWV infectivity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic Arabidopsis expressing NSs, auxin biosynthetic and response genes are reduced
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; IAA quantification by LC-MS/MS; RT-qPCR; Y2H; GST pull-down; Co-IP; BiFC; split-LUC; Y3H; infectious clone assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces IAA accumulation and auxin signaling, thereby promoting TSWV infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression of auxin likely affects plant growth/development; NSs transgenics showed altered phenotypes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of TSWV-infected Arabidopsis, accession NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin supports plant growth/development and antiviral defense responses
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSs suppresses expression of YUC auxin biosynthetic genes and lowers IAA levels. 2. NSs physically interacts with TCP17 in the nucleus. 3. NSs disrupts TCP17 dimerization and transcriptional activation of YUC promoters, promoting viral infection.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Encoded during Barley stripe mosaic virus infection as a silencing suppressor
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights TCP17 as a conserved antiviral target; breeding for TCP17 variants resistant to multiple viral suppressors may provide broad-spectrum resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shown to interact with TCP17 and inhibit TCP17 dimerization; overexpression of TCP17 reduced BSMV accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Y3H; agroinfiltration with BSMV γb-GFP infectious clone; GFP and immunoblot assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely suppresses TCP17-mediated auxin biosynthesis/signaling to facilitate BSMV infection
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for TCP17 transient expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BSMV γb interacts with TCP17. 2. γb inhibits TCP17 self-dimerization. 3. TCP17 overexpression reduces BSMV accumulation, supporting biological relevance of this targeting strategy.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Encoded during Rice stripe virus infection as a silencing suppressor
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports targeting TCP17 as a broad-spectrum engineering node against multiple RNA viruses
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shown only for interaction with TCP17 and disruption of TCP17 dimerization; no direct metabolite measurement in this system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RSV NS3 interacts with TCP17. 2. NS3 inhibits TCP17 dimerization in Y3H assays. 3. This supports a conserved viral strategy targeting host auxin regulation.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Encoded during Tomato bushy stunt virus infection as a silencing suppressor
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports TCP17-centered resistance engineering against diverse viral suppressors
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shown only for interaction with TCP17 and disruption of TCP17 dimerization; no direct metabolite measurement in this system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TBSV P19 interacts with TCP17. 2. P19 inhibits TCP17 dimerization in Y3H assays. 3. This supports conservation of viral targeting of auxin regulatory machinery.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis overexpression and knockout analysis; RT-qPCR; IAA quantification by LC-MS/MS; dual-luciferase promoter activation assays; EMSA; Y2H; GST pull-down; Co-IP; BiFC; split-LUC; Y3H; virus infection assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Targeted by viral suppressors/effectors NSs, γb, NS3, and P19 during infection
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 2×35S constitutive promoter; YUC2/YUC5/YUC8 native promoters in reporter assays; DR5 synthetic auxin-responsive promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Auxin synthesis-related transcription factor from Arabidopsis; promotes YUCCA expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates YUC2, YUC5, and YUC8 transcription; enhances auxin-responsive genes including IAA3, SAUR22, and IAA29 indirectly via increased IAA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In overexpression lines, elevated TCP17 leads to increased auxin biosynthesis and signaling gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds YUC5 promoter specifically; NSs does not block TCP17 DNA binding directly but inhibits TCP17 homodimerization and transactivation; partial redundancy with other TCPs likely masks stronger tcp17 mutant effects; NSs does not interact with TCP5/TCP13/TCP8/TCP22 in this study; orthologous activation of SlYUC5 and CaYUC5 by SlTCP17 and CaTCP17 suggests conservation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA biosynthesis and auxin signaling, enhancing resistance to TSWV and BSMV
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused visible developmental phenotype changes in Arabidopsis, consistent with altered auxin status
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin regulates plant growth/development and contributes to defense against viral infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms TCP17 homodimers; viral proteins disrupt this self-associated complex
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSWV infection; BSMV infection; exogenous IAA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key transcription factor for host auxin synthesis; decisive because viral effectors converge on TCP17 to suppress YUC expression and lower IAA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCP17 directly activates YUC2, YUC5, and YUC8 promoters and increases IAA accumulation. 2. TCP17 overexpression elevates auxin-responsive gene expression and enhances resistance to TSWV and BSMV, whereas tcp17 mutants are more susceptible to TSWV. 3. Multiple viral suppressors interact with TCP17 and inhibit its homodimerization, blocking its activation of auxin biosynthesis genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for broad-spectrum antiviral engineering; overexpression or editing to create virus-insensitive TCP17 alleles may sustain auxin-mediated defense, though developmental pleiotropy from auxin elevation should be managed
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of TSWV-infected Arabidopsis used to identify auxin biosynthesis gene suppression, accession NA
